falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Signals-first control plane as a blanket default
falsificationQueries: Temporal updates vs signals acknowledgement official docs
Temporal update with start GA operator control
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Signals alone are too weak for acknowledged operator actions. Updates are now mature enough that the blueprint should distinguish best-effort nudges from validated control operations instead of avoiding Updates wholesale.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Convex append-only attempt ledger is obviously safe and cheap
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: An external ledger is still justified, but only if it is scoped, deduped, and stores blob references or hashes where necessary. Raw full-body storage by default could create unnecessary Convex pressure.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: DB-first outbox is always the right start-consistency pattern
falsificationQueries: Temporal signal with start update with start consistency
Temporal start workflow in transaction guidance
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Outbox is not automatically required. With-Start primitives plus idempotent workflow IDs may be sufficient for some flows, so the blueprint must narrow where an outbox is actually justified.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Visibility can be trusted for automation-grade correctness
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Visibility remains useful for discovery but is still too weak as a sole correctness oracle. The blueprint should keep stronger per-execution truth or a projection path for automation.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Temporal is clearly best by default with no meaningful runtime caveats
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Temporal remains the strongest default, but only with Node workers and an explicit versioning plan. Bun support is still experimental, and alternatives are not obviously better on the current evidence.
